metric_definitions.strict_criterion: iPAE < 7/31, pLDDT > 0.90, and binder scRMSD < 1.5 A, evaluated as paired per-candidate metrics.
metric_definitions.strict_global_unique_count: Globally deduplicated binder sequences that satisfy the strict benchmark criterion within the whole run.
metric_definitions.tree_snapshot: Observed search nodes with recorded intent/hypothesis and measured outcome. Internal ranking mechanics are intentionally omitted from the public view.
metric_definitions.x_axis: Accumulated Proteina-Complexa GPU-hours only. LLM/controller wall time is not included.

nodes.hypothesis: child_003 (diverse-beam) was a near-miss (iPAE 0.242). The failure was likely due to insufficient interface packing refinement. By adding sequence_hallucination (which worked for MCTS) and increasing diversity_weight (0.5 -> 0.8) and beam width (4 -> 8), we aim to force the search to explore more diverse interface geometries that can be refined into stable binders. The active path multiplier is 8*4=32. Work product: 8*4*32 = 1024. This hits the cap exactly, which is allowed.
nodes.intent: Explore/Rescue the promising diverse-beam branch by combining it with the successful refinement strategy (sequence_hallucination) and increasing diversity parameters to escape the local optimum that caused the near-miss.
nodes.observed: added 2 globally new strict unique candidates; best iPAE 0.185, best pLDDT 0.955, best binder scRMSD 0.057
nodes.title: diverse-beam-search

nodes.hypothesis: Reducing MCTS simulations from 32 to 16 increases the number of independent search trees per GPU hour, potentially exploring different regions of the sequence space while retaining the successful sequence hallucination refinement. This tests if the success is robust to lower simulation depth or if deeper search is strictly required.
nodes.intent: Local variant of child_004 to test sensitivity to n_simulations and increase throughput of independent trials.
nodes.observed: added 1 globally new strict unique candidates; best iPAE 0.197, best pLDDT 0.957, best binder scRMSD 0.060
nodes.title: mcts+sequence_hallucination

nodes.hypothesis: MCTS with sequence hallucination is the most efficient operator family (4 successes). Reducing n_simulations from 32 to 16 satisfies the max_expanded_work_product_cap (16 * 16 * 4 = 1024) while maintaining the successful strategy. This exploits the high-yield region identified in child_004 and child_006.
nodes.intent: Exploit the proven MCTS+SH strategy from child_004/006, adjusted for budget constraints.
nodes.observed: added 1 globally new strict unique candidates; best iPAE 0.202, best pLDDT 0.943, best binder scRMSD 0.067
nodes.title: mcts+sequence_hallucination

nodes.title: stochastic-beam-search

nodes.hypothesis: child_000 (beam-search) had the lowest iPAE (0.289) but used low noise (0.1). The 'near_miss_interface' suggests the model is stuck in a local optimum with insufficient interface specificity. Increasing sc_scale_noise to 0.5 (moderate diffusion noise) while widening the beam to 8 (active_path_multiplier=16) encourages the generation of structurally distinct interface candidates that may achieve lower iPAE than the conservative low-noise search.
nodes.intent: Local variant of child_000. Keep the beam-search algorithm but perturb the noise parameter significantly to escape the local interface optimum that caused the near-miss. This tests if the backbone can support a different, more confident interface.
nodes.observed: added 0 globally new strict unique candidates; best iPAE 0.466, best pLDDT 0.880, best binder scRMSD 0.398
nodes.title: beam-search

nodes.hypothesis: child_003 (diverse-beam) showed good geometry (low binder_scRMSD) but poor iPAE/pLDDT. The current portfolio lacks diversity in search strategy outcomes. By increasing diversity_weight to 0.8 and beam_width to 8 (active_path_multiplier=16), we force the search to explore orthogonal regions of sequence/structure space. This rescue lane aims to find a completely different binding motif that might naturally satisfy the iPAE threshold, avoiding the local optima trapping the beam and MCTS searches.
nodes.intent: Explore/Rescue. Pivot to a high-diversity search strategy to escape the 'hard target' plateau. Previous diverse-beam attempt (child_003) used lower diversity weight (0.5) and narrower beam (4). This proposal aggressively maximizes diversity to find unique scaffolds.
nodes.observed: added 0 globally new strict unique candidates; best iPAE 0.402, best pLDDT 0.903, best binder scRMSD 0.264
nodes.title: diverse-beam-search

nodes.hypothesis: Zero success without a clear near-miss signal suggests broadening search stochastically under the same bounded compute envelope.
nodes.observed: added 0 globally new strict unique candidates; best iPAE 0.813, best pLDDT 0.863, best binder scRMSD 0.435
nodes.title: stochastic-beam-search

nodes.hypothesis: Stochastic beam search failed completely. Diverse beam search (child_007) showed moderate promise (iPAE 0.402) but lacked aggressive interface optimization. By combining high diversity weight (0.9) and parent penalty (0.5) with sequence hallucination refinement weighted towards iPAE, we aim to find structurally distinct binders that escape the current local optimum.
nodes.intent: Explore/Rescue using Diverse Beam Search. Diverge from child_007 by increasing diversity_weight (0.8->0.9) and adding parent_penalty (0.5) to actively penalize convergence to the 'KIS' motif, while enabling sequence hallucination for interface refinement.
nodes.observed: added 0 globally new strict unique candidates; best iPAE 0.285, best pLDDT 0.931, best binder scRMSD 0.068
nodes.title: search

nodes.hypothesis: The backbone from child_009 is structurally sound (pLDDT > 0.91) but the sequence is suboptimal for interface packing (iPAE ~0.285). Applying bounded sequence hallucination with a high weight on iPAE loss should refine the side-chain packing to lower the predicted alignment error, addressing the 'near_miss_interface' failure mode directly.
nodes.intent: Local variant of child_009 adding sequence refinement to fix the specific iPAE bottleneck identified in the near-miss analysis.
nodes.observed: added 1 globally new strict unique candidates; best iPAE 0.206, best pLDDT 0.946, best binder scRMSD 0.072
nodes.title: refinement

nodes.hypothesis: child_009 achieved the best metrics (iPAE 0.285) but failed the strict threshold. The 'KIS' motif collapse suggests local optima trapping. Increasing diversity_weight and parent_penalty while introducing moderate backbone noise (sc_scale_noise 0.2) should perturb the interface packing just enough to escape the local minimum without destabilizing the high-confidence backbone.
nodes.intent: Exploit the child_009 backbone class by refining search parameters to break sequence degeneracy and improve interface confidence (iPAE).
nodes.observed: added 0 globally new strict unique candidates; best iPAE 0.719, best pLDDT 0.883, best binder scRMSD 0.362
nodes.title: search

nodes.hypothesis: child_010 is the only successful lineage, using diverse-beam-search (diversity_weight=0.9) followed by sequence_hallucination refinement. Repeating this exact configuration exploits the known success basin to find additional unique binders. The high diversity weight prevents collapse, while refinement fixes interface geometry.
nodes.intent: Exploit the single successful lineage (child_010) to maximize cumulative unique successes. This is a direct replication of the winning strategy to ensure robustness and find more variants in the same structural basin.
nodes.observed: added 0 globally new strict unique candidates; best iPAE 0.247, best pLDDT 0.931, best binder scRMSD 0.110
nodes.title: refinement

nodes.hypothesis: child_010 succeeded using diverse-beam-search (width 8, branch 2, div 0.9, pen 0.5) followed by sequence_hallucination refinement. Replicating this exact configuration exploits the known success path to maximize the probability of finding additional unique binders in the same high-probability region of sequence space.
nodes.intent: Exploit the single confirmed success path (child_010) to generate more unique successes. We maintain the exact search parameters that yielded the global best iPAE (0.206) and apply the same refinement strategy that converted a near-miss scaffold into a success.
nodes.observed: added 0 globally new strict unique candidates; best iPAE 0.293, best pLDDT 0.911, best binder scRMSD 0.111
nodes.title: refinement
